expected_properties: clk_div toggles at least twice within a larger observation window, demonstrating continued operation
additional_cases.goal: Demonstrate that the divided clock continues to toggle repeatedly after initial observation
notes: Window size is conservative (e.g., 40 clock edges) to avoid reliance on exact NUM_DIV value
preconditions: Reset has been released and system is stable
stimulus_intent: Maintain reset deasserted and let the design run for a longer period
timing_assumptions: Monitor clk_div for at least 40 consecutive clk cycles

expected_properties: Asserting reset while clk_div is toggling forces clk_div to return to a known post‑reset state
additional_cases.goal: Verify correct reset behavior when asserted asynchronously during normal operation
notes: Reset is asserted after a few observed toggles of clk_div, then released; output should stabilize without X or glitches
preconditions: System has observed at least one toggle of clk_div
stimulus_intent: Assert rst_n low asynchronously during operation, then release it and observe clk_div stabilization
timing_assumptions: Allow a few clock cycles after reset release before checking stable state

expected_properties: clk_div toggles at least once within a conservative observation window after reset release
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Confirm basic functional progress of clk_div after reset without assuming exact cycle counts
notes: Observation window is chosen conservatively (e.g., 20 clock edges) to accommodate unknown division factor
stimulus_intent: Release reset and observe clk_div over several clock cycles
timing_assumptions: Observe over a conservative number of clock edges (e.g., 20) after reset deassertion
